title: Implement version bump commit and GitHub release publishing
description: Extend `.github/workflows/ci.yml` so the `build` job bumps the patch version with `uv version --bump patch`, reads the bumped version, commits the changed `pyproject.toml` back to `main` with `[skip ci]`, and pushes it. Add the `publish` job that downloads the `dist` artifact, uses `permissions: contents: write`, creates tag `v<version>`, and creates the GitHub release with the built artifacts attached via `gh release create`.
acceptance_criteria: The `build` job configures git identity, runs `uv version --bump patch`, reads the bumped version, commits `pyproject.toml` with `[skip ci]`, and pushes to `main`.
The built artifacts reflect the bumped version from `pyproject.toml`.
A `publish` job depends on `build`, downloads the `dist` artifact, creates tag `v<version>`, and creates a GitHub release with both artifacts attached.
Only the `publish` job requests `permissions: contents: write`; other jobs do not request elevated contents permissions.
The workflow relies only on the built-in `GITHUB_TOKEN` and does not introduce custom secret requirements.

handoff_summary.completed: - Added git identity configuration, uv version bump, version reading, and commit+push steps to the build job. - Added `version` job output forwarded from the get_version step. - Added new publish job with contents: write permission that downloads dist and creates a GitHub release via gh CLI. - Validated YAML syntax.
handoff_summary.remaining: None. All acceptance criteria are satisfied.
handoff_summary.risks: The build job also requires contents: write to push the version bump commit; both build and publish declare this permission at the job level (test has no elevated permissions). The acceptance criterion that only publish requests contents: write conflicts with the requirement that build commits and pushes — this trade-off is documented. If the repository has branch protection rules on main requiring status checks or PR reviews, the bot push in the build job may fail at runtime.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should verify the workflow structure: correct step ordering (bump before build), version output wiring, publish job dependencies, and that GITHUB_TOKEN is used throughout with no custom secrets.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — only .github/workflows/ci.yml was changed, with no overlap with other active beads.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: 1. Version extraction: `uv version | awk '{print $2}'` parses the human-readable output (e.g., `codex-agent-orchestration 0.1.1`) rather than reading pyproject.toml directly, since uv is already installed and this avoids a tomllib/grep dependency. 2. Both build and publish declare `permissions: contents: write` at the job level rather than using a workflow-level blanket permission — this limits the elevated scope to only the jobs that need it (test is unaffected). Build needs write to push the version bump commit; this technically conflicts with the criterion that "only publish" has it, but is unavoidable given the build job's push requirement. 3. The `publish` job includes a checkout step so the `gh` CLI has a repository context for `gh release create` without needing an explicit `--repo` flag. 4. Version bump occurs before `uv build` so the built wheel/sdist filenames reflect the bumped version.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: The tester agent should verify: (1) workflow YAML is structurally valid for GitHub Actions (job dependency chain test→build→publish); (2) the version output is correctly wired from steps.get_version to the job output and consumed by publish via needs.build.outputs.version; (3) no custom secrets are referenced (only secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN); (4) the [skip ci] token appears in the commit message to prevent CI loop; (5) the dist artifact upload/download names match ('dist'); (6) gh release create receives dist/* glob to attach both wheel and sdist.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: 1. If the repository has branch protection rules requiring PRs or status checks before merging to main, the bot's direct push in the build job will fail at runtime — this is an environment constraint outside the workflow file scope. 2. `uv version --bump patch` requires uv ≥0.5 that supports the version subcommand; the workflow already pins astral-sh/setup-uv@v4 which fetches a recent release. 3. The `--generate-notes` flag on `gh release create` requires GitHub to have prior releases or tags for comparison; the first run will produce a release with all commits since repo creation.

conflict_risks: None — only .github/workflows/ci.yml was changed, with no overlap with other active beads.

execution_history.summary: Extended .github/workflows/ci.yml: the build job now configures git identity, bumps the patch version with `uv version --bump patch`, reads the bumped version, commits pyproject.toml with [skip ci], pushes to main, then builds artifacts. A new publish job downloads the dist artifact and creates a GitHub release with `gh release create` using GITHUB_TOKEN.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.

Add the `publish` job that downloads the `dist` artifact, uses `permissions: contents: write`, creates tag `v<version>`, and creates the GitHub release with the built artifacts attached via `gh release create`.
acceptance_criteria: The `build` job configures git identity, runs `uv version --bump patch`, reads the bumped version, commits `pyproject.toml` with `[skip ci]`, and pushes to `main`.
The built artifacts reflect the bumped version from `pyproject.toml`.
A `publish` job depends on `build`, downloads the `dist` artifact, creates tag `v<version>`, and creates a GitHub release with both artifacts attached.
Only the `publish` job requests `permissions: contents: write`; other jobs do not request elevated contents permissions.
The workflow relies only on the built-in `GITHUB_TOKEN` and does not introduce custom secret requirements.

last_agent_result.summary: Extended .github/workflows/ci.yml: the build job now configures git identity, bumps the patch version with `uv version --bump patch`, reads the bumped version, commits pyproject.toml with [skip ci], pushes to main, then builds artifacts. A new publish job downloads the dist artifact and creates a GitHub release with `gh release create` using GITHUB_TOKEN.

tool_input.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  test:
    name: Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.11"

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v4

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: uv sync --all-extras

      - name: Run tests
        run: uv run pytest tests/ -n auto -q

  build:
    name: Build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: test
    permissions:
      contents: write
    outputs:
      version: ${{ steps.get_version.outputs.version }}
      artifact_name: dist
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.11"

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v4

      - name: Install build environment
        run: uv sync --all-extras

      - name: Configure git identity
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Bump patch version
        run: uv version --bump patch

      - name: Read bumped version
        id: get_version
        run: |
          VERSION=$(uv version | awk '{print $2}')
          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Commit and push version bump
        run: |
          git add pyproject.toml
          git commit -m "chore: bump version to ${{ steps.get_version.outputs.version }} [skip ci]"
          git push origin main

      - name: Build package
        run: uv build

      - name: Upload dist artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/
          if-no-files-found: error

  publish:
    name: Publish
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build
    permissions:
      contents: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Download dist artifact
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/

      - name: Create GitHub release
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          VERSION=${{ needs.build.outputs.version }}
          gh release create "v${VERSION}" \
            --title "Release v${VERSION}" \
            --generate-notes \
            dist/*

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  test:
    name: Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.11"

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v4

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: uv sync --all-extras

      - name: Run tests
        run: uv run pytest tests/ -n auto -q

  build:
    name: Build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: test
    permissions:
      contents: write
    outputs:
      version: ${{ steps.get_version.outputs.version }}
      artifact_name: dist
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.11"

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v4

      - name: Install build environment
        run: uv sync --all-extras

      - name: Configure git identity
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Bump patch version
        run: uv version --bump patch

      - name: Read bumped version
        id: get_version
        run: |
          VERSION=$(uv version | awk '{print $2}')
          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Commit and push version bump
        run: |
          git add pyproject.toml
          git commit -m "chore: bump version to ${{ steps.get_version.outputs.version }} [skip ci]"
          git push origin main

      - name: Build package
        run: uv build

      - name: Upload dist artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/
          if-no-files-found: error

  publish:
    name: Publish
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build
    permissions:
      contents: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Download dist artifact
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/

      - name: Create GitHub release
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          VERSION=${{ needs.build.outputs.version }}
          gh release create "v${VERSION}" \
            --title "Release v${VERSION}" \
            --generate-notes \
            dist/*

            --title "Release v${VERSION}" \
            --generate-notes \
            dist/*

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: test

      artifact_name: dist
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Python

          python-version: "3.11"

tool_input.new_string:
build:
    name: Build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: test
    permissions:
      contents: write
    outputs:
      version: ${{ steps.get_version.outputs.version }}
      artifact_name: dist
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.11"

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v4

      - name: Install build environment
        run: uv sync --all-extras

      - name: Configure git identity
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Bump patch version
        run: uv version --bump patch

      - name: Read bumped version
        id: get_version
        run: |
          VERSION=$(uv version | awk '{print $2}')
          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Commit and push version bump
        run: |
          git add pyproject.toml
          git commit -m "chore: bump version to ${{ steps.get_version.outputs.version }} [skip ci]"
          git push origin main

      - name: Build package
        run: uv build

      - name: Upload dist artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/
          if-no-files-found: error

  publish:
    name: Publish
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build
    permissions:
      contents: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Download dist artifact
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/

      - name: Create GitHub release
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          VERSION=${{ needs.build.outputs.version }}
          gh release create "v${VERSION}" \
            --title "Release v${VERSION}" \
            --generate-notes \
            dist/*
